result: Loaded audit directive. Scope: ITool, IModule, ModuleTool/Executor, ToolRegistry, ModuleRegistry, ParameterDefinition, ToolType, 14 BasicTools, serialization surface.

action: Glob **/*.cs across Tools, Core, Modules, BasicTools, LlmProviders

finding: Interface has 5 properties (Id, Name, Description, Type, Metadata) and 1 method (ExecuteAsync). No input schema property. No output schema. Metadata is IReadOnlyDictionary<string,object> — untyped.

Interface has 5 properties (Id, Name, Description, Parameters as IReadOnlyDictionary<string,ParameterDefinition>, Tools as IReadOnlyCollection<ITool>) and 2 methods (InitializeAsync, ExecuteAsync). Has typed parameter schema. No output schema.

5 fields: Name (string, required), Description (string, required), Type (string, required — free-form not enum), Required (bool, default true), DefaultValue (object?, nullable). MISSING: AllowedValues, ExampleValues, enum constraints.
5 values: None, Native, External, Custom, Module. No Browser, FileData, UIDesktop, UIBrowser distinction. Playwright (browser) uses External same as API. UIA uses Native same as file tools.

Methods: Register, TryGetTool, Unregister, Clear. Property: Tools (IReadOnlyDictionary<string,ITool>). FormatForSystemPrompt() returns plain text '- {id}: {description}' per tool — not JSON, not structured. No JSON export. No descriptor type.
Methods: Register, TryGetModule, Unregister, Clear. Property: Modules. NO FormatForSystemPrompt. NO export methods. Only enumeration via .Modules property.

Wraps IModule as ITool. Id/Name/Description forwarded from module. Type fixed to ToolType.Module. Metadata = module.Parameters.ToDictionary(p=>p.Key, p=>(object)p.Value) — ParameterDefinition objects stored as object, type erased for consumers.

Methods: ExecuteAsync (by moduleId), GetAll() -> IReadOnlyDictionary<string,IModule>. Can enumerate all modules. Thin delegation layer — no schema or metadata surfacing beyond what ModuleRegistry provides.

ResolveTool(id) looks up in ToolRegistry, then ModuleRegistry (wraps in ModuleTool via factory), then nested module tools. ExecuteToolAsync wraps result in ExecutionResult. No schema enumeration methods.

Description: populated (inline text listing params). Parameters: operation (read|write), filePath, content (write only) — all read from raw dictionary, no ParameterDefinition. Metadata: SupportedExtensions=['.json']. No formal input/output schema.
Description: populated. Parameters: operation (read|write|append), filePath, content — raw dictionary. Metadata: SupportedExtensions=['.txt','.log']. No formal schema.

Description: populated. Parameters: operation (read|write|getsheets|readcell|writecell), filePath, sheetName, data, cellRef, value — raw dictionary. Metadata: SupportedExtensions=['.xlsx']. No formal schema.

Description: populated. Parameters: action (create|delete|list|copy|move|exists), path, destPath, recursive, pattern — raw dictionary. Metadata: SupportedActions=[enum names from FolderAction]. FolderAction enum has 6 values. No formal ParameterDefinition.

Description: populated. Parameters: method (GET|POST|PUT|DELETE|PATCH), url, body, headers — raw dictionary. Metadata: SupportedMethods=[enum names from ApiAction]. Has SSRF protection. Output includes StatusCode, Body, Headers — informal (no schema).

Description: populated (free-text listing actions and pipe format). Input: single 'input' parameter or separate action/element/url/value params. Metadata: Platform='Windows', SupportedActions=[UIAutomationAction enum names]. 14 actions. Conditional compile #if WINDOWS. Action variants in Description free text only.

Description: richest of all tools — full markdown table of actions embedded as C# raw string literal. 14 actions. Metadata: Platform='Cross-platform', SupportedActions=[BrowserAction enum names]. Conditional compile #if PLAYWRIGHT_ENABLED. EvaluateJs action unique to Playwright. No formal schema.

Can RECEIVE tool calls from LLMs (parses Anthropic tool_use and OpenAI tool_calls response formats). BuildRequestBody does NOT include tool/function definitions in request. NO outbound tool schema generation. No ITool-to-LLM-schema conversion method anywhere in codebase.

action: Confirmed by exploration agent: jsonl-tool, jsonc-tool, csv-tool, tsv-tool, psv-tool, xml-tool, word-tool all follow identical pattern to json-tool — Description string populated, parameters accessed via raw dictionary, no ParameterDefinition, no output schema.

rationale: Description surface is present on all 14 tools and IModule has typed ParameterDefinition. However 4 BLOCKING gaps (no ITool input schema, no ToolDescriptor type, no LLM schema conversion, UI action variants not in schema) mean THIRAVIYAN cannot assemble a complete ARIVULAR-consumable tool context from existing surface alone. Moderate extensions to Valaiorp contracts are needed before integration is viable.
